titleStmt.title: SII 11.5: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami

No. 5. (B.K. No. 197 of 1928-29). The record has since been published by Mr. N. Lakshminaravan Rao, M.A.,Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XXI, pp. 204. ff. [It is also possible to construe that Koṇḍiśulara Kuppa founded the Kīrtivarmagōsāsi (sa ?) (evidently named so in honour of the king) in front of the temple.—Ed.]. AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A PILLAR SET UP IN FRONT OF THE BANAŚAṄKARĪ TEMPLE.

summary.p: The record belongs to the reign of king Kīrtivarman II and is dated in the 6th year of his reign. It commemorates the construction of a chēdiya (Jain temple) by Kaliyamma, the gāmuṇḍa of Jēbuḷagēri and the erection of a sculpture in front of it by Koṇḍiśulara Kuppa alias Kīrtivarma-gōsāsi. Regarding gōsāsi, see Ep. Ind. Vol. VI, p. 255.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . kīrttivarmma satyāśraya
śrī prithuvīvallabha mahārājā
dhirāja paramēśvara bhaṭārara
rājyaṁ Ondu ttaramabhivṛddhi sa
li Āṟaneyā varṣamprava

rttamānamāge jī
buḷagērige kali
yamma gāmuṇḍugeydī
cēdiyamānmāḍisidān
Idaṟamunda koṇḍi

śulara kuppa kīrttivarmma
gōsāsiyā niṟisidā
kīrttanaṁ © diśāpālasya li
khitaṁ©prabhunāman©
Digital edition of SII 11.5 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
